automated_grading.note: Automated keyword grading has known false-failure rate (~15%) due to keyword extraction limitations. Qualitative scores are the reliable signal.

analyst_observations: Skills produce a consistent +0.88 quality uplift (4.93 vs 4.05 on 5-point scale) across 10 evals spanning 7 domains.

Skills win 9/10 evals. The one loss (Reg BI rollover, -0.25) is on a topic well-represented in base training data. Even there, the skill structured the response better while missing an edge case (NUA) that has since been added to the skill.

The automated grader paradoxically shows without-skill ahead (81% vs 76%) due to keyword extraction false failures. This highlights that keyword-based assertions cannot replace qualitative review for knowledge skills.
Largest skill uplift (+1.25) is on multi-domain questions (rebalance + TLH) requiring coordinated knowledge across tax law, portfolio math, and compliance rules.

Consistent +1.0 uplift across all 6 new operational/advisory evals, driven by domain-specific frameworks (NIGO management, STP tiered processing, custodian billing windows, regulatory mitigation hierarchies).
Token overhead is 54% (reading SKILL.md files). This is the cost of the knowledge injection.

Without-skill responses occasionally contribute concepts the skills miss: FATCA/CRS tax analysis, CAPM/Treynor metrics, NSCC interface details, NUA treatment. These are expansion opportunities for the skills.
